ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, fully legible. ICC Certificate of Origin issued by West Sweden Chamber of Commerce.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_notes: Clear text-based PDF, 3 pages. Issued by Vitrolife Sweden AB. All text fully legible.

lc_check.motivation: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number SEG-25D-326856 does not contain an LC bank term reference. No field labeled 'L/C No.', 'Letter of Credit', 'Documentary Credit' or similar found.

locations_found: Invoice page 1 footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'
Invoice page 2 footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'
Invoice page 3 footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'
Invoice page 3 signature block: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Gustaf Werners gata 2, SE-421 32 Västra Frölunda, Sweden'

pairing_validation.motivation: All three pairing criteria are met: (1) Certificate references invoice 2005654, invoice bears number -2005654 (same number with prefix dash); (2) Consignor 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' identified in invoice footer and header; (3) Consignee 'Luvian company' identified in both delivery address and invoice address fields.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 42132 Göteborg, Sweden
checkpoints.invoice_value: Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN (footer all pages); Vitrolife (header/logo all pages); Vitrolife Sweden AB, Gustaf Werners gata 2, SE-421 32 Västra Frölunda, Sweden (page 3 signature block)

comparison_steps.description: Searching for certificate consignor name 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' in all invoice pages.
comparison_steps.detail: Exact match found in invoice footer on all pages: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'. Also found in page 3 signature block: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB'. Header logo shows 'Vitrolife' brand.

comparison_steps.description: Verifying that the consignor company name in the certificate matches the invoice issuer.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB'. Invoice footer: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB'. Exact match after normalization.
Certificate states Sweden. Invoice footer explicitly states 'SWEDEN'. Additionally, VAT number SE556546629801 has SE prefix confirming Sweden. Fully verified.

rules_applied.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name found explicitly in invoice footer/header.
Name verification – exact match of legal party name.
Country verification via explicit address and VAT prefix.

checkpoints.motivation: The consignor 'Vitrolife Sweden AB' in the certificate is explicitly and exactly identified in the invoice footer on all three pages as 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'. The country Sweden is confirmed by both the explicit address in the footer and the SE-prefix in the VAT number SE556546629801. MATCH per rules 4.1.0 and 4.1.2.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor: 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 42132, Göteborg, Sweden'. Invoice issuer (footer): 'Vitrolife Sweden AB, Box 9080, 400 92 Göteborg, SWEDEN'. Company name is an exact match. Country Sweden verified via explicit 'SWEDEN' in footer and SE-prefix in VAT. Minor difference in postal code format (42132 vs 400 92) is irrelevant per rule 4.1.5 which states address differences do not cause MISMATCH.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Luvian company, Vital Village - Villa No.55, Zhulian Sharifi, 44001 Erbil, Iraq
checkpoints.invoice_value: Invoice address: Luvian Company, vital village house number 55, IQ-Erbil, Iraq; Delivery address: Luvian company, Vital Village - Villa No.55, IQ-44001 Erbil, Iraq

comparison_steps.step: 4.2.0.2 – Priority identification of consignee in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Identifying the primary billing field in the invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice has an 'Invoice address' field (primary billing field) containing 'Luvian Company, vital village house number 55, IQ-Erbil, Iraq'. This is the authoritative consignee per rule 4.2.0.2.

comparison_steps.description: Comparing certificate consignee name with invoice address name.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate: 'Luvian company'. Invoice address: 'Luvian Company'. After case normalization: 'luvian company' = 'luvian company'. Exact match.

rules_applied.description: Priority order for consignee identification – Invoice address (billing field) has priority.
Address differences do not cause MISMATCH when name and country match.

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate consignee 'Luvian company' is identified in the invoice's 'Invoice address' field as 'Luvian Company'. After case normalization, these are identical. Country Iraq matches exactly. The address differs slightly ('Vital Village - Villa No.55' vs 'vital village house number 55') but per rule 4.2.4, address differences do not cause MISMATCH when name and country match. MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee: 'Luvian company, Vital Village - Villa No.55, Zhulian Sharifi, 44001 Erbil, Iraq'. Invoice 'Invoice address': 'Luvian Company, vital village house number 55, IQ-Erbil, Iraq'. Name: 'Luvian company' vs 'Luvian Company' → identical after case normalization. Country: Iraq = Iraq. Address format differs but rule 4.2.4 explicitly states address differences do not cause MISMATCH.

checkpoints.certificate_value: Reproductive Media Kits for IVF, See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730
checkpoints.invoice_value: Various IVF-related products (G-TL, G-MOPS PLUS, G-IVF PLUS, EmbryoGlue, G-PGD, RapidVit Cleave, RapidWarm Cleave, etc.) with declaration: 'Componets for culturing media & medical instruments for In Vitro Fertilisation IVF'

comparison_steps.description: Checking if certificate contains article numbers.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate does not contain specific article numbers. Proceeding to Priority 2/3.

comparison_steps.step: 4.3.5 Priority 3 – Generic description with invoice reference
comparison_steps.description: Certificate uses generic description 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF' with explicit invoice reference 'See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730'.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate contains: (1) generic goods description 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF', (2) explicit invoice reference 'See attached invoice 2005654', (3) date '20250730'. Invoice number: -2005654 (normalized: 2005654) matches. Invoice shipping date: 20250731. Certificate date references 20250730. Date difference is exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1, a 1-day difference results in MANUAL_REVIEW — however, checking further: the invoice also has 'Our order no: 2005654' which matches exactly. The date '20250730' in the certificate may refer to the order date or invoice creation date vs the shipping date 20250731. Applying rule 4.3.5.1: date discrepancy within 1 day → MANUAL_REVIEW for date. However, the invoice content is fully consistent with the certificate description: all items are IVF reproductive media products (G-TL, G-MOPS PLUS, G-IVF PLUS, EmbryoGlue, RapidVit, OVOIL, ICSI, SpermRinse, HYASE etc.) and the invoice declaration explicitly states 'Componets for culturing media & medical instruments for In Vitro Fertilisation IVF'. The goods are fully consistent.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate references invoice 2005654. Invoice bears number -2005654 / Our order no 2005654.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate: '2005654'. Invoice 'Invoice no': '-2005654' (dash prefix is formatting, normalized: 2005654). Invoice 'Our order no': '2005654'. Exact match.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate references date 20250730. Invoice shipping date 20250731.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate date: 20250730 (2025-07-30). Invoice shipping date: 20250731 (2025-07-31). Difference is exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1, this should result in MANUAL_REVIEW. However, checking if the date 20250730 can be found elsewhere in the invoice: the 'Due date' field shows '20250830' which does not match. No exact match for 20250730 in the invoice. Applying rule 4.3.5.1: 1-day discrepancy → MANUAL_REVIEW.

comparison_steps.description: Verifying that invoice content is consistent with certificate description 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF'.
comparison_steps.detail: All invoice items are IVF reproductive media products. Invoice footer declaration: 'Componets for culturing media & medical instruments for In Vitro Fertilisation IVF'. Fully consistent.

rules_applied.description: Generic goods description with explicit invoice reference – invoice number and date verified.
Date discrepancy within 1 day – should result in MANUAL_REVIEW.

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate uses a generic goods description 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF' with explicit reference 'See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730'. This activates rule 4.3.5 (generic description with invoice reference). Invoice number 2005654 matches exactly. However, the date 20250730 does not exactly match the invoice shipping date 20250731 — the difference is exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1, a 1-day date discrepancy results in MANUAL_REVIEW, not MISMATCH. The invoice goods content is fully consistent with 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF'. Applying 4.3.5.1: the date discrepancy triggers MANUAL_REVIEW for this checkpoint.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: The certificate date reference '20250730' and invoice shipping date '20250731' differ by exactly 1 day. Rule 4.3.5.1 explicitly states this should result in MANUAL_REVIEW. However, reconsidering: the invoice number match is exact (2005654), the goods are fully consistent, and the 1-day difference is the sole issue. Per rule 4.3.5.1 the checkpoint status should be MANUAL_REVIEW due to the date.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 contains 'Gross: 66,40 kg'. Box 6 contains '4 cartons'. Per rule 4.4.2.1, box 7 is the verification-grounding quantity.
comparison_steps.detail: Box 7 value: Gross 66,40 kg. This is the primary verification quantity. Box 6 also provides '4 cartons' as a secondary quantity (package count).

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.1 – Occurrence check for box 7 value (Gross 66,40 kg)
comparison_steps.description: Searching for 66.40 or 66,4 in the invoice as gross weight.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice states 'Gross weight (kg): 66,4'. Certificate states 'Gross: 66,40 kg'. Applying allowed decimal truncation: 66.40 truncated to one decimal = 66.4. Alternatively, 66,4 = 66,40 (trailing zero). These represent the same numerical value. Weight category: Certificate says 'Gross', invoice says 'Gross weight'. Both are gross weight. Unit: Certificate says 'kg', invoice says '(kg)'. Match.

comparison_steps.step: 4.4.3.1 – Check for contradictions with other quantities
comparison_steps.description: Invoice also states Net weight (kg): 35,5. Certificate does not state a net weight value, so no contradiction.
comparison_steps.detail: Certificate only states gross weight. Invoice states both gross and net weight. No net weight contradiction since certificate does not claim a net weight value.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 6 states '4 cartons'. Invoice states 'Number of cartons: 4'.
comparison_steps.detail: Both state 4 cartons. This is a secondary verification point, further confirming the match.

rules_applied.description: Occurrence check – numerical value identified in invoice.
Verification against invoice – numerical value and unit match.
No contradictions with other quantity values in invoice.

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate box 7 states 'Gross: 66,40 kg'. Invoice states 'Gross weight (kg): 66,4'. These represent the same numerical value (66.40 = 66.4 with trailing zero difference). Weight category is 'Gross' in both. Unit is 'kg' in both. Additionally, certificate states '4 cartons' and invoice states 'Number of cartons: 4' — confirming match. Invoice also states Net weight 35,5 but certificate does not claim a net weight, so no contradiction per rule 4.4.3.1. MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Primary verification: Certificate Gross 66,40 kg vs Invoice Gross weight (kg) 66,4. Values: 66.40 vs 66.4 — identical (trailing zero). Category: Gross = Gross weight. Unit: kg = kg. All three components match. Secondary: 4 cartons = Number of cartons 4. No contradictions found.

comparison_steps.description: Searching for origin indication in invoice.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice page 1 contains 'Country of Origin: Sweden' in the product table header area. This is an explicit origin field.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate states 'European Community'. Invoice states 'Sweden'. Sweden is an EU member state.
comparison_steps.detail: Per rule 4.5.4.1, 'European Community' is equivalent to 'European Union' / 'EU'. Per rule 4.5.4.2, if the certificate states EU/European Community and the invoice states a specific EU member state, the origin is verified. Sweden is an EU-27 member state (ISO: SE). Therefore, 'European Community' in certificate is verified by 'Sweden' in invoice.

comparison_steps.step: 4.5.4.1 – Check for non-EU origins in invoice
comparison_steps.description: Checking if invoice contains any non-EU origin countries.
comparison_steps.detail: Invoice states only 'Country of Origin: Sweden' as origin. No non-EU origins found. No contradiction.

rules_applied.description: EU normalization – 'European Community' treated as equivalent to EU.
EU ↔ member state exchange – certificate says EU/EC, invoice says Sweden (EU member) → verified.

checkpoints.motivation: Certificate states origin 'European Community'. Invoice states 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Per rule 4.5.4.1, 'European Community' is equivalent to EU. Per rule 4.5.4.2, when the certificate states EU and the invoice states a specific EU member state, the origin is verified. Sweden is an EU-27 member state. No non-EU origins are present in the invoice. MATCH.
checkpoints.debug_explanation: Certificate origin: 'European Community' (box 3). Invoice origin: 'Country of Origin: Sweden' (explicit origin field on page 1). EU normalization rule 4.5.4.2 explicitly allows the exchange: cert=EU ↔ invoice=EU member state → verified. Sweden is EU member (SE). No non-EU origins in invoice. Result: MATCH.

rule_evaluation_log.detail: No LC reference found in certificate.
All three criteria met: invoice number match, consignor identified, consignee identified.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Koncernstruktur – consignor name search in invoice
rule_evaluation_log.detail: Vitrolife Sweden AB found in invoice footer on all pages.
Sweden confirmed via explicit address and SE-prefix VAT number.
Invoice address field identified as primary billing field with 'Luvian Company'.
Luvian company = Luvian Company after case normalization.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Address differences do not cause MISMATCH
rule_evaluation_log.detail: Address format differs but name and country match.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Generic goods description with invoice reference
rule_evaluation_log.detail: Certificate uses generic description with explicit invoice reference 2005654.
Certificate date 20250730 vs invoice shipping date 20250731 — exactly 1 day difference. Rule states this should give MANUAL_REVIEW.
66.40 kg gross found as 66.4 kg gross weight in invoice.
Invoice net weight 35.5 not contradicted by certificate (certificate doesn't claim NW).
Certificate: EU (European Community). Invoice: Sweden (EU member). Verified.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Invoice date 20250730 not found – date MISMATCH
rejected_hypotheses.reason: The certificate references date 20250730 and the invoice shipping date is 20250731 — a difference of exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1, a 1-day discrepancy does not constitute MISMATCH but triggers MANUAL_REVIEW. The goods description checkpoint should technically be MANUAL_REVIEW due to this date discrepancy.
Rejected per EU normalization rules 4.5.4.1 and 4.5.4.2. European Community/EU is a collective designation, and Sweden is an EU member state. The exchange is explicitly permitted in both directions.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: Postal code mismatch (42132 vs 400 92) could indicate different party
rejected_hypotheses.reason: Rejected. Different postal code formats for Göteborg. Rule 4.1.5 states address differences do not cause MISMATCH for consignor when name and country match. The company name is an exact match.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Avsändare (Vitrolife Sweden AB), mottagare (Luvian company, Irak), kvantitet (66,40 kg brutto) och ursprungsland (European Community/Sverige) stämmer alla överens mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Varubeskrivningen 'Reproductive Media Kits for IVF' verifieras via fakturareferens 2005654, men datumet i certifikatet (2025-07-30) avviker med en dag från fakturans leveransdatum (2025-07-31), vilket kräver manuell kontroll.
blocking_issues: Goods description checkpoint: certificate references date 20250730 but invoice shipping date is 20250731 — 1-day discrepancy triggers MANUAL_REVIEW per rule 4.3.5.1.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.motivation: Name 'Luvian company' matches 'Luvian Company' after case normalization. Country Iraq matches. Address differences are irrelevant per rule 4.2.4.

sections.certificate_value: Reproductive Media Kits for IVF, See attached invoice 2005654 dated 20250730
sections.invoice_value: Invoice no -2005654, Shipping date 20250731; Various IVF products; Declaration: 'Componets for culturing media & medical instruments for In Vitro Fertilisation IVF'
sections.rule_applied: Generic goods description with invoice reference (4.3.5). Invoice number matches. Date discrepancy of 1 day triggers MANUAL_REVIEW per 4.3.5.1.
sections.motivation: Invoice number 2005654 matches exactly. Certificate date 20250730 vs invoice shipping date 20250731 — difference of exactly 1 day. Per rule 4.3.5.1, this triggers MANUAL_REVIEW. Goods content is fully consistent (all items are IVF reproductive media).

sections.rule_applied: Occurrence check – gross weight value and unit match exactly. Package count matches.
sections.motivation: Certificate gross 66,40 kg = invoice gross weight 66,4 kg (trailing zero difference). Both state gross weight in kg. 4 cartons matches. No contradictions with net weight (certificate doesn't claim NW).

sections.rule_applied: EU normalization – European Community ≈ EU. Sweden is EU member state → verified per EU↔member state exchange rule.
sections.motivation: Certificate states 'European Community' (equivalent to EU). Invoice states 'Country of Origin: Sweden'. Sweden is an EU-27 member state. Per rule 4.5.4.2, when certificate says EU and invoice says an EU member state, origin is verified. No non-EU origins in invoice.

output_quality_checks.notes: Goods description checkpoint is MANUAL_REVIEW due to 1-day date discrepancy (rule 4.3.5.1). This triggers overall MANUAL_REVIEW per section 6.1 (at least one checkpoint cannot be definitively resolved). All other checkpoints are MATCH with high confidence.
